Article Title: Krunal Pandya: IPL 2026: One trick at a time, Krunal Pandya shows why skill, not match-ups, matters
Article Text:
Krunal Pandya (PTI Photo)

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

IPL is changing the landscape of cricket, and Krunal is changing the landscape of finger-spin bowling RCB batting coach and mentor Dinesh Karthik

Poll Which situation do you think Krunal performs best in? Death overs Middle overs Initial overs

NEW DELHI: If taking pitch, conditions, dimensions, and match-ups out of the equation is an art, then Krunal Pandya is a Picasso. As the imbalance between bat and ball continues to tilt heavily in favour of the former in IPL 2026, the left-armer has managed to restore some normalcy whenever he has had the ball in hand. With his unique bag of tricks and a street-smart brain, Krunal has stayed one step ahead, one variation at a time.At a time when teams obsess over matchups, prioritising them over skill, the Royal Challengers Bengaluru don't face that problem with Krunal. Left-hander, no problem. In-form opener, no problem. Death overs? No problem. He is well-equipped to handle any situation, and the ease with which he has operated in different phases of the game speaks volumes about the calm head he possesses. Even when Axar Patel and Ravindra Jadeja are being underbowled due to the match-up obsessions, Krunal has continued to do the heavy lifting for the RCB in IPL 2026, even at the unforgiving Chinnaswamy.Axar bowled Nitish Rana's part-time off-spin when the SRH left-handers were on song. RR skipper Riyan Parag doesn't seem to trust the experienced Jadeja, but Rajat Patidar clearly has the weight behind Krunal. The RCB all-rounder has bowled 25 overs in 7 games, while Jadeja has bowled only 15 in 8, and Axar has bowled 23 in 7. Yes, Krunal's economy is higher compared to the other two seasoned finger spinners, but his lion-heartedness has helped him stand out from the pack.Take, for instance, the away fixture vs the Rajasthan Royals when young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was firing on all cylinders. Krunal not only got rid of the dangerman but also surprised the new man, Shimron Hetmyer, with a well-directed bouncer - something he has been consistently and accurately doing this season. It's not just the bouncer. He mixes his pace, uses different release points, plays around the popping crease and nails yorkers with the precision of a fast bowler.On the eve of their match against the Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026, RCB batting coach and mentor Dinesh Karthik — who reinvented the batting wheel during his "finishing" days with the franchise — praised Krunal for "pushing the boundaries".“He is a player that this game is going to remember for a long time because he is doing something that has not been done before. He’s pushing the boundaries of what a finger-spinner means,” Karthik told reporters on Sunday.For Krunal, it's not just about being different but being very shrewd in what he does. The bouncers are not bowled to look cool — he has his braids for that — but to "create a doubt in the batter". Like he successfully did against his now teammate Venkatesh Iyer, who played for the Kolkata Knight Riders last season. The bouncer forced the left-hander to ask for a helmet, and the next delivery caught him napping and crashed into the woodwork.“He’s bowling deliveries that people really couldn’t fathom a few years ago, and he’s using them very cleverly. He’s not someone who’s just doing it because it looks cool. He knows that it does create a doubt in the batter. He is a trend-setter. IPL is changing the landscape of cricket, and Krunal is changing the landscape of finger-spin bowling,” Karthik added.Krunal's last appearance for the country was in 2021 when he played the T20I vs Sri Lanka in Colombo. Since then, he has played for different IPL franchises and continued to grind in the domestic circuit for his state, Baroda, but, most importantly, he has striven to stay one step ahead of batters. In a chat with JioHotstar, the all-rounder expressed his strong desire to wear the Indian jersey again, and the 35-year-old doesn't need to look far for comeback inspiration. RCB's mentor, and Krunal's biggest cheerleader, is no stranger to comebacks.
Article Summary: Krunal Pandya is changing the landscape of finger-spin bowling, says RCB batting coach and mentor Dinesh Karthik. The left-armer has managed to restore some normalcy whenever he has had the ball in hand. Krunal's economy is higher compared to the other seasoned finger spinners, but his lion-heartedness has helped him stand out from the pack.

Article Title: Ipl 2026: IPL 2026 security scare: 240 CCTV cameras sabotaged during RCB vs GT match
Article Text:
Image credit: BCCI/IPL

Watch Krunal Pandya on RCB’s performance and key turning points Krunal Pandya on RCB’s performance and key turning points

Who filed the complaint?

What was the reason behind the damage?

Poll Should there be stricter security measures during major events like IPL matches? Yes No

NEW DELHI: In a shocking development, the CCTV surveillance system at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium was damaged on the day of the IPL 2026 match between Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Gujarat Titans on April 24, according to a report by Deccan Herald.The report stated that more than 240 cameras were found non-functional, disrupting surveillance across key security areas on match day.Despite the chaos, defending champions RCB successfully chased down Gujarat Titans’ massive 205/3 in 18.5 overs.Cubbon Park police acted quickly and booked two individuals - Manjunath (37), a resident of Hiriyur in Chitradurga, and Abdul Kalam (19), from Uttar Pradesh."The incident took place on April 24 during the match. Both the accused were working under a sub-vendor of IVS Digital Solutions, providing digital services during match days," a senior police officer said.As per the report, Aditya Bhat, an employee of Staqu Technologies Pvt Ltd, a Gurugram-based firm that provides AI-driven surveillance solutions during match days, lodged a complaint with the police.He alleged that Manjunath and Abdul entered the CCTV control room without valid passes. He also accused them of damaging the Network Video Recorder (NVR) systems and related fibre connections.CCTV footage reportedly shows the duo accessing restricted areas.Due to the disruption, officials were unable to provide live surveillance feeds to police personnel deployed at the stadium.During the investigation, the accused reportedly told police that they had not been paid dues amounting to Rs 10 lakh.
Article Summary: CCTV surveillance system at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium was damaged on the day of the IPL 2026 match between Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Gujarat Titans. More than 240 cameras were found non-functional, disrupting surveillance across key security areas on match day.

Article Title: Angkrish Raghuvanshi: IPL 2026: Angkrish Raghuvanshi out explained — should Rishabh Pant have withdrawn the appeal?
Article Text:
Angkrish Raghuvanshi in the middle (IPL Photo)

Watch Ashok Sharma story: Inside the village and house which shaped India's new pace sensation Ashok Sharma story: Inside the village and house which shaped India's new pace sensation

IPL 2026 : Should Rishabh Pant have withdrawn the appeal?



Poll Who do you think played a bigger role in KKR's recent victory? Sunil Narine Rinku Singh

NEW DELHI: Angkrish Raghuvanshi’s controversial dismissal for obstructing the field during Kolkata Knight Riders’s (KKR) clash against Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) on Sunday has sparked a wider debate, not just about the law, but also the “spirit of the game” and whether LSG skipper Rishabh Pant should have withdrawn the appeal.The incident occurred when Raghuvanshi, attempting a quick single, was sent back and dived to make his ground as a throw came in from the infield. The ball struck him mid-dive, leading to an appeal from Mohammed Shami. After referral, the third umpire ruled him out, citing a change in running line.As per IPL playing conditions (Clause 37.1.4), a batter can be given out if he “significantly changed his direction without probable cause and thereby obstructed a fielder’s attempt to effect a run out,” regardless of whether a run-out was likely.Speaking to Cricbuzz after the match, former India cricketer Rohan Gavaskar disagreed with the decision, saying, “I think it's not out becuase when the ball came and the batter dived, he was not looking at the ball.“He was looking to make that dive. If he was looking at the ball, then he wouldn't have dived, knowing that if he had dived, the ball would hit his body. When he made that dive, it means that it was unintentional.So I would think that it was not out.”The bigger question, however, was whether Pant, as captain, could have withdrawn the appeal.Former Indian cricketer Virender Sehwag weighed in, “When you are winning, you, as the captain, think that we can let go of this. But since Lucknow were not winning, then they thought that that we could get this wicket, and we would get benefit. So they appealed and as per law, it is a correct appeal.“But we can say that in spirit of the game, they should not have done it. But when you have made a law, then where was spirit of the game?“But I will say that this was third umpire's decision, and he was watching it on the TV. And when he put in the dive, it's not that he was looking at the ball... so it was indeed a harsh call.”In the game, Sunil Narine and Rinku Singh starred as Kolkata Knight Riders edged Lucknow Super Giants in a thrilling Super Over in IPL 2026. Narine’s double strike restricted LSG, while Rinku sealed the win with a boundary.The victory lifted KKR off the bottom of the points table after a tense contest in Lucknow.
Article Summary: Angkrish Raghuvanshi was dismissed for obstructing the field during Kolkata Knight Riders’s clash against Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) on Sunday. The incident has sparked a wider debate, not just about the law, but also the “spirit of the game” and whether LSG skipper Rishabh Pant should have withdrawn the appeal.

Article Title: ‘Bhaiya nahi, brother’: Rinku Singh's family call with Sunil Narine steals the show - Watch
Article Text:
Rinku Singh's family call with Sunil Narine (Screengrabs)

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

Poll Who was the standout player in KKR victory over LSG? Rinku Singh Sunil Narine Nicholas Pooran Aiden Markram

NEW DELHI: Rinku Singh had match-winning impact both with the bat and on the field to help Kolkata Knight Riders pull off a thrilling victory over Lucknow Super Giants in IPL 2026 on Sunday.After the match, a light-hearted moment between Rinku, his family, and Narine caught fans’ attention.During a video call, Rinku’s sister greeted Narine by saying, “Hello bhaiya”. Rinku quickly corrected her, asking her to say “brother” instead since Narine doesn’t understand Hindi. Despite that, she continued praising him in Hindi, saying, “Bahut acchi bowling kari aapne. (You bowled really well)”Rinku then translated her words, leading Narine to respond with a smiling “Thank you very much”. The fun exchange left everyone laughing and quickly went viral online.Watch:KKR were in deep trouble at 93/7 when Rinku stepped in and turned the game around with an unbeaten 83 off 51 balls, guiding his team to 155/7. LSG matched the total, pushing the contest into a tense Super Over.There, Sunil Narine delivered under pressure, conceding just one run and taking two key wickets. KKR then chased the target on the very first ball, with Rinku hitting the winning runs to seal a memorable victory.Narine proved to be the game-changer in the Super Over, dismissing Nicholas Pooran and Aiden Markram to restrict LSG to just one run, the lowest-ever total in a Super Over.Rinku wasn’t just brilliant with the bat; he also impressed in the field, taking four catches and staying involved throughout the game. His performance earned him the Player of the Match award and capped off a remarkable all-round display in a high-pressure encounter.
Article Summary: Rinku Singh had match-winning impact both with the bat and on the field. Kolkata Knight Riders pulled off a thrilling victory over Lucknow Super Giants. Rinku's sister greeted Narine by saying, “Hello bhaiya” during a video call.

Article Title: IPL 2026: Rishabh Pant told to give up LSG captaincy, Ex-India cricketer names replacement
Article Text:
Rishabh Pant (PTI Photo)

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

Poll Who do you think should lead LSG if they change captain? Aiden Markram Another player Stick with Rishabh Pant

NEW DELHI: Rishabh Pant ’s struggles as captain of Lucknow Super Giants continued after another tough loss, this time against Kolkata Knight Riders in a Super Over.LSG failed to chase 156, adding to a worrying run of five straight defeats this season. Their home record is even worse, with eight consecutive losses under Pant’s leadership since last year. LSG have lost 14 of their last 22 matches under Pant.Former India batter Wasim Jaffer believes a change in leadership could help the team turn things around. He suggested that Aiden Markram should take over as captain and even laid out a preferred batting order for the side.Pant’s captaincy struggles have also reflected in his batting. Since last season, he has scored just 458 runs in 21 innings, with only three half-centuries. Even in the latest match, his 42 wasn’t enough to guide the team home.Jaffer feels Pant’s natural attacking game is being affected by the added responsibility.“I feel a player like Rishabh Pant should play purely as a player. You get the best out of him when you free him up and tell him to just go out and play and win matches on his own. Don’t burden him with captaincy. I think Aiden Markram is a better captain," Jaffer said on his YouTube channel."He has led South Africa very successfully, he is mentally strong, very composed, and I think he can get the best out of the other players. But this doesn’t look like something that will actually happen. This is just my suggestion, but if I were there, that’s what I would do."Jaffer didn’t hold back while assessing the team’s overall performance either.“LSG looks completely out of place right now, it’s hard to even know what to expect from them. If I were in that dugout, the first thing I would do is make Aiden Markram the captain… That would be my top seven, and I would go with this batting order,” he said.
Article Summary: LSG have lost 14 of their last 22 matches under Rishabh Pant. Former India batter Wasim Jaffer believes a change in leadership could help the team turn things around. He suggested that Aiden Markram should take over as captain and even laid out a preferred batting order.

Article Title: Virat Kohli's silent impact: How modern T20 power-hitting builds on his template
Article Text:
Virat Kohli (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

Poll Should Virat Kohli be considered the gold standard for T20 batting in India? Yes, absolutely No, there are better players It's subjective, depends on personal preference

NEW DELHI: There are only a handful of games that Virat Kohli will play on his home turf in Delhi. That explains the deafening clamour for passes for Royal Challengers Bengaluru’s (RCB) weekday match against Delhi Capitals in the corridors of the Delhi and District Cricket Association (DDCA).From brash non-conformist youngster to a spiritually driven statesman, the legend of Kohli is a story of constant evolution. It’s a process that doesn’t cease, one that inspires awe in people who witness it firsthand. It has allowed him to go shoulder-to-shoulder with a younger generation that is rewriting the grammar of T20 batting, a process that helps him retain the superstardom even when an anti-superstar culture has been initiated in Indian cricket.IPL has transcended to a level where it is close to being dubbed as a different sport to what is being played around the world. On Sunday, a staggering 986 runs were scored in 77.2 overs in two matches. The use of unabated power, force and physicality behind those runs could match the violence of any action movie.At the other end of the spectrum is Kohli who uses his bat as a fine piece of equipment that shreds the morale of the bowling attacks bit by bit, over by over.Match by match.Even as the world was recovering from Sunday’s gory run fest — that saw 265 and 229 being comfortably chased down — one must recall that it was Kohli who played the lead role in a comfortable 200-plus chase against Gujarat Titans an evening before. Gold standard in batting in Indian cricket for the last 15 years, his legacy has quietly influenced the renaissance of India’s contemporary T20 batting.The funky ramp shots, the scoops and the audacious reverse sweeps/scoops are barely seen in T20 cricket now. RCB captain Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal—RCB’s engine room in the middle overs—have redefined themselves as T20 batters this year banking on conventional shot-making. They spent the entire evening here at the Kotla, hitting big shots down the ground.“I remember a few years ago, there used to be a lot of these scoops and other funky shots. Now, it’s about how players can just check their drives. I would say people are focusing more on making sure their normal shots that they have a lot more power added to it in clearing the boundary,” RCB mentor Dinesh Karthik said on Sunday.That Kohli is still challenging the next-gen of India’s T20 batting stars for the Orange Cap (highest run-scorer) is a testimony to his insatiable hunger for excellence. Ironically, this is the format that he first quit in international cricket two years ago when questions were raised on his ability to keep up with the times.And now when the cricket world has embraced scores of 225 and beyond as IPL’s new normal, Kohli has stayed relevant and a perfect example for his peers around him.RCB mentor Dinesh Karthik puts it down to the man’s stubbornness to trust his methods. “He goes back every year and assesses what he has done. He comes back with a very stubborn plan and he gets it done not on match days alone but the way he practices as well,” Karthik said.“The youngsters in the team are constantly asking me what Virat bhaiya is doing differently,” Karthik mentioned, “Credit to him that he constantly rediscovers himself with every passing year and for him to keep up with the pace of this game where some of the newer players are batting at insane strike rates.”
Article Summary: IPL has transcended to a level where it is close to being dubbed as a different sport to what is being played around the world. Virat Kohli has quietly influenced the renaissance of India’s contemporary T20 batting. RCB captain Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal have redefined themselves as T20 batters this year.

Article Title: “They are deeply connected”: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini’s drama takes a new turn amid claims of unresolved emotions between them
Article Text:
Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini’s drama takes a new turn amid claims of unresolved emotions between them.(Image via Getty Images)

Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini might still be feeling a few things for each other as their alleged affair sparks outrage



Whats going on between Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini?



Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini remain the talk of the town even as they try to disappear from the public eye amid their alleged affair rumors.As Mike Vrabel seeks some time off to be with his family as he faces a difficult time in his personal life, Dianna Russini has deleted her account on X.A few hours ago, as per Daily Mail, a source revealed that, “There is still a deep connection between Dianna and Mike that they can’t act on now that they are surrounded by this controversy…”The source added, “The next several months are going to be a humongous test for both of them.”While many have expected the rumored couple to be going their separate ways owing to their respective spouses, it seems that might not be the case.As per Daily Mail, the source said that, “literally every option seems to be on the table,” which even might include the two separating from their respective spouses and giving their alleged relationship a chance.However, nothing has been confirmed yet as both Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini remain distant as their affair rumors have sparked outrage among millions.Interestingly, when their photos from the upscale hotel were leaked to the media, both Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini were quick to shut down things as the head coach even dismissed the photos as “laughable”.However, the rumors refused to die down as Dianna Russini was sidelined from her job at The Athletic and a few days later, she stepped down from her prestigious role.Now, there have been new claims of Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini being involved in an alleged affair since 2020 as their pictures from a New York City bar have sparked chaos.To make matters worse, in the 2020 pictures from their alleged date, Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini were spotted sharing a kiss and it has sparked outrage.Both Mike Vrabel’s wife and Dianna Russini’s husband have stayed silent as they maintain distance from all the rumors about the alleged affair drama.
Article Summary: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini might still be feeling a few things for each other as their alleged affair sparks outrage. A source revealed that, “There is still a deep connection between Dianna and Mike that they can’t act on now that they are surrounded by this controversy…” The next several months are going to be a humongous test for both of them.

Article Title: IPL 2026: Lungi Ngidi's first words after horrific injury in DC vs PBKS clash
Article Text:
Lungi Ngidi (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

Watch Ravindra Jadeja picks the key turning point in Rajasthan Royals win Ravindra Jadeja picks the key turning point in Rajasthan Royals win

How Lungi Ngidi got injured during DC vs PBKS clash

Poll Should more attention be given to player mental health post-injury? Absolutely, it's important No, they should focus on recovery

NEW DELHI: Lungi Ngidi has finally broken his silence after suffering a frightening injury during the IPL 2026 clash between Delhi Capitals and Punjab Kings at the Arun Jaitley Stadium, assuring fans that he is recovering well.The South African pacer took to Instagram to thank supporters for their concern and issued an update on his condition."Thank you for the messages, all good," Ngidi wrote in his first public reaction after the injury scare, easing fears over his condition, while sharing a story on Instagram.Ngidi has since been discharged from a Delhi hospital after the incident. The fast bowler suffered a worrying fall on the back of his head and neck during the match and was taken to BLK-Max Super Speciality Hospital for observation.Delhi Capitals co-owner and chairman Kiran Kumar Grandhi also visited the hospital to check on the pacer’s condition.Despite KL Rahul’s sensational 152*, a record-breaking 126-run opening stand between Prabhsimran Singh and Priyansh Arya, and a blistering 71* from skipper Shreyas Iyer, Punjab Kings pulled off a historic chase of 265 at the Arun Jaitley Stadium.Lungi Ngidi suffered a frightening on-field injury during the IPL 2026 clash between Delhi Capitals and Punjab Kings, leaving him momentarily unconscious and bringing the match to a complete halt as players from both sides looked visibly shaken.The incident took place in the third over of Punjab Kings’ innings, bowled by Axar Patel, when Priyansh Arya lofted a shot towards the deep. Ngidi, stationed in the outfield, judged it well and moved backwards to attempt what appeared to be a routine catch.However, as he stretched while going back, he lost his balance and failed to get even a fingertip to the ball. In the process, he landed heavily on the back of his head and neck, triggering immediate alarm on the field.Medical staff rushed in quickly as players from both teams gathered around. The game was halted for over 10 minutes while Ngidi received on-field treatment before being stretchered off and taken to hospital in an ambulance for further evaluation.The severity of the fall prompted immediate concern, with Vipraj Nigam brought in as a concussion substitute. Despite the scary incident, the IPL later confirmed that Ngidi was stable and expected to be discharged.He was admitted to BLK-Max Super Speciality Hospital in Delhi and later discharged after observation. Delhi Capitals co-owner Kiran Kumar Grandhi also visited the hospital to check on his condition.
Article Summary: Lungi Ngidi has been discharged from a Delhi hospital after the incident. The fast bowler suffered a worrying fall on the back of his head and neck during the match and was taken to BLK-Max Super Speciality Hospital for observation. The South African pacer took to Instagram to thank supporters for their concern.

Article Title: Babar Azam: Babar Azam 'bowled' by Shaheen Afridi's 1-year-old son; fans react - WATCH
Article Text:
Babar Azam (Image credit: PSL)

Watch Why Pakistan don't trust Babar Azam any more | T20 World Cup 2026 Why Pakistan don't trust Babar Azam any more | T20 World Cup 2026

NEW DELHI: Babar Azam may be dominating the Pakistan Super League this season, but he recently found himself at the receiving end of a light-hearted moment involving Shaheen Afridi ’s one-year-old son, Aliyar.Babar, currently the second-highest run-scorer in PSL 2026, has been in stellar form with 485 runs in nine innings at an impressive average of 80.83 and a strike rate of 141.40. However, a viral video from the tournament showed him being playfully “bowled” by young Aliyar, leaving the star batter momentarily stunned while Afridi burst into laughter.The clip quickly spread across social media, drawing humorous reactions from fans."Bobzy getting owned by a kid lol," one fan wrote."Bachay say bhi beat hogya," another user joked.The moment came after the clash between Babar’s Peshawar Zalmi and Afridi’s Lahore Qalandars at the Gaddafi Stadium.On the field, Lahore Qalandars registered a six-wicket win to keep their playoff hopes alive.Despite the defeat - their first of the season - Peshawar Zalmi continue to sit comfortably at the top of the table with eight wins from 10 matches and 17 points.Lahore Qalandars, meanwhile, climbed to fourth place with five wins and five losses, taking their tally to 10 points and staying in contention for a playoff spot.
Article Summary: Babar Azam is the second-highest run-scorer in PSL 2026. A viral video shows him being playfully "bowled" by young Aliyar. Peshawar Zalmi continue to sit comfortably at the top of the table.

Article Title: Sourav Ganguly's father Chandidas passes away
Article Text:
Former India captain Sourav Ganguly's father Chandidas Ganguly passed away after a prolonged illness. He was 73 and is survived by wife Nirupa and two sons.

KOLKATA: Former India captain Sourav Ganguly's father Chandidas Ganguly passed away after a prolonged illness. He was 73 and is survived by wife Nirupa and two sons, first-class cricketer Snehasish and Sourav.Ganguly senior was seriously ill with high blood sugar and was given insulins regularly while being treated at home."His condition deteriorated in the evening and was rushed to the MP Birla Heart Research centre. He passed away around 6.30 pm, while being on the way," CAB joint secretary Sujan Mukherjee said.His body will be brought to CAB for final homage before the funeral.Ganguly senior had played a significant role in shaping the career of both his sons Snehasish and Sourav.As a kid Sourav would accompany his elder brother Snehasish to Eden Gardens as their father put them under the tutelage of current Saurashtra coach Debu Mitra."I don't have words to express. It's really a very sad moment for me. I really don't know what to say," an upset Debu Mitra said after hearing the news.Ganguly senior also sent Sourav to play Minor County under the watchful eyes of Mitra in England during the late 1980s as his cricketing career got off after getting into the triumphant Ranji Trophy side in 1989-90 under Sambaran Banerjee.He had served Cricket Association of Bengal in various capacities starting as assistant secretary in 1974-75.He went on to become treasurer, secretary, vice-president and member of trustee board but he stopped going to CAB after Ganguly was dropped from the Test side in 2005.
Article Summary: Ganguly senior was seriously ill with high blood sugar and was given insulins regularly while being treated at home. Ganguly senior had played a significant role in shaping the career of both his sons Snehasish and Sourav. He had served Cricket Association of Bengal in various capacities.

Article Title: ‘My new favourite player’: Cummins in awe of teen sensation Sooryavanshi
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (PTI Photo)

Watch IPL 2026: RR assistant coach Vikram Rathour speaks after loss to KKR IPL 2026: RR assistant coach Vikram Rathour speaks after loss to KKR

JAIPUR : On a cricket field, certain names demand caution. But for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, reputations are merely obstacles between him and the boundary.On Saturday night at the Sawai Mansingh Stadium, the Rajasthan Royals opener showed once again that he plays the ball, not the man - regardless of how many World Cups that man may have won.The carnage began in the very first over, as he tore into Praful Hinge with breathtaking disdain. Four consecutive sixes set the tone — the first launched into the stands, the second pulled over deep square leg, the third flicked to long leg and the fourth carved over long-on.He didn’t slow down. In the next over, he welcomed Pat Cummins with an imperious six over deep midwicket - a statement shot that underlined his fearlessness. Where most young batters might have taken time to settle, Sooryavanshi needed just one ball to assert himself.This wasn’t a one-off. Earlier in the tournament, he had shown the same audacity against Jasprit Bumrah and Josh Hazlewood. It has quickly become a hallmark of his game — whether facing India’s premier pacer or Australia’s finest, Sooryavanshi attacks with the same intent.His 37-ball 103 powered Rajasthan Royals to a formidable 228.But it wasn’t enough, as Sunrisers Hyderabad chased down the target with five wickets in hand.Even in defeat, Sooryavanshi left a lasting impression. Cummins, the opposition captain, was effusive in his praise.“He’s my new favourite player. He hits the ball so hard. It’s great to watch, it’s good fun,” Cummins said at the post-match press conference. “You’ve got to be right on the money as a bowler, because if you’re not, it’s going a long way. He’s impressive. He’s had a great start to his career and I like the way he plays. He takes the game on,” he added.For Sooryavanshi, the hundred was simply an outcome of a clear, uncomplicated mindset - play with freedom.“I don’t have a plan for a first-ball six. Before the match, we planned to play freely and back our game. The wicket was good, so I trusted myself. I wanted to give my 100% for the team,” he said during the innings break.“I thought about the shots I could play and tried to visualise them.”At 15, Sooryavanshi isn’t just announcing himself - he’s redefining how fearlessly the next generation might approach the game.
Article Summary: 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi hit four sixes in the first over. His 37-ball 103 powered Rajasthan Royals to a formidable 228. But it wasn't enough, as Sunrisers Hyderabad chased down the target.

Article Title: Ipl: IPL 2026: KKR down LSG in Super Over as Rinku Singh's 83* trumps Mohsin Khan's 5/23
Article Text:
Image credit: BCCI/IPL

Watch Justin Langer on LSG batting failure and opens up on the team's bowling Justin Langer on LSG batting failure and opens up on the team's bowling

LUCKNOW: Sunil Narine has fought and won countless battles for Kolkata Knight Riders over the years. On Sunday night at the Ekana Stadium, the veteran once again stood at the centre of chaos, entrusted with bowling the first Super Over of this IPL in a finish that swung violently till the final delivery.Lucknow Super Giants, who had clawed their way back to force the tie, erred immediately by sending an out-of-form Nicholas Pooran to open the Super Over alongside Aiden Markram. Narine flighted the first ball, bowled it slower through the air, and rattled Pooran’s stumps to decisively tilt the contest.Markram followed three balls later, dismissed on the boundary as Rovman Powell and Rinku Singh combined to complete a superb catch under pressure. With just two runs to defend, Rinku needed only one ball, calmly striking it away to seal KKR’s second win of the season.Earlier, chasing 156, LSG endured a roller-coaster pursuit. Brief contributions from Rishabh Pant and Ayush Badoni offered hope, but wickets continued to stall momentum. With 17 required in the final over, Kartik Tyagi bowled two costly waist-high no-balls and conceded sixes, yet still had seven to defend off the final ball. Mohammed Shami, however, held his nerve, dispatching a length delivery into the stands to force the Super Over.The defeat will sting LSG fast bowler Mohsin Khan the most. The left-armer delivered a sensational spell of 5/23, nearly dragging his side home by restricting KKR to 155/7 despite a defiant 83 from Rinku Singh (51 balls).Mohsin ripped through the top order early, dismissing Tim Seifert for a duck and removing captain Ajinkya Rahane for 10 in successive maiden overs. The innings took an extraordinary turn when Angkrish Raghuvanshi became only the fourth batter in IPL history to be given out obstructing the field, reacting angrily as he walked off.With KKR struggling at 31/4, Cameron Green and Rinku steadied things before Mohsin struck again. Rinku then launched a late counter-attack, adding 62 off 29 balls with Narine, including four consecutive sixes in the final over, to give KKR a fighting total, one that ultimately proved just enough in a night where Narine’s calm proved decisive once more.Brief Scores: Kolkata Knight Riders 155/7 (Rinku Singh 83 not out, Cameron Green 34; Mohsin Khan 5/23) tied with LSG 155/8 (A Markram 31, R Pant 42, A Badoni 24; V Arora 2/24, V Chakravarthy 2/33). Super Over LSG 1/2 in 0.3 overs lost to KKR 2/0 in 0.1 over.
Article Summary: Kolkata Knight Riders beat Lucknow Super Giants by two wickets in IPL clash. KKR won by one wicket in the final over of the match at the Ekana Stadium. Rinku Singh scored 83 from 51 balls to give KKR a fighting total.

Article Title: Csk Vs Gt: IPL 2026: CSK crumble in Powerplays as Gujarat Titans script eight-wicket win
Article Text:
Image credit: BCCI/IPL

Watch ‘We haven’t played good cricket’ - Mahela Jayawardene after MI’s biggest IPL loss vs CSK ‘We haven’t played good cricket’ - Mahela Jayawardene after MI’s biggest IPL loss vs CSK

CHENNAI: The stark contrast in Powerplay efficiency proved decisive as Gujarat Titans dismantled Chennai Super Kings by eight wickets at the MA Chidambaram Stadium on Sunday.The stars of the show were South African paceman Kagiso Rabada (3-25), who broke the back of the CSK top-order, and in-form ‘home boy’ B Sai Sudharsan (87,46 balls; 4x4, 7x6), whose orthodox strokeplay propelled GT to fifth on the table with eight points. CSK now have six from eight and their hopes of a last-four berth are fast diminishing.The tone was set early when the Titans’ pace duo of Rabada and Mohammed Siraj ripped through the hosts with a ferocious opening spell on a rare lively Chepauk surface, restricting them to a paltry 28-3 in the Powerplay. On the other hand, GT’s openers mastered the two-paced conditions, during their chase of a tricky 159. Captain Shubman Gill (33) and Sudharsan helped the Titans race to 55-0 in the first six overs, effectively seizing control despite a disciplined opening from Anshul Kamboj, who conceded just 3 runs in his first two overs.Sudharsan continued his stellar run this season and remained unfazed even after losing Gill in the seventh over. His innings, marked by control and clean striking, was studded with seven sixes and four fours. The youngster and the No. 3 batter Jos Buttler, hardly put a foot wrong and went on to stitch a 97-run partnership for the second wicket. With CSK’s attack offering little resistance, the duo turned the chase into a formality, finishing it off with 20 balls to spare.However, Rababa and Siraj set up the win under the sweltering sun. They bent their backs, got bounce off the Chepauk surface, occasionally pitching one up, making it difficult for the CSK openers Sanju Samson and Ruturaj Gaikwad.Rabada’s clinical double-strike, pounding in at 148–149 kph, removed the man in form Samson and Urvil Patel, playing his first game of this IPL. Siraj followed up with a cross-seamer to send back the ‘Impact Player’ Sarfaraz Khan for a golden duck, and suddenly skipper Ruturaj Gaikwad (74*, 60b; 4x6, 6x4) was watching his side crumble before the Powerplay had even run its course.The nightmare only deepened as the bowlers forced a suffocating dry spell. There were no boundaries for five overs, and the pressure mounted when left-arm spinner Manav Suthar dismissed Dewald Brevis just as he looked to break the shackles. By the time CSK crawled to 50 in 12 overs, they had recorded their second-slowest team fifty in IPL history and the slowest by any side this season.With the innings stalling at a precarious 43-3 at the halfway stage, Gaikwad finally took charge and played a gritty knock. He began shifting gears slowly and stitched together a recovery with useful cameos from Shivam Dube, Kartik Sharma, and Jamie Overton. En route, he brought up a much-needed half-century -his first in 11 IPL innings and his first this season -on a surface that remained far from easy for batters.In a frantic penultimate over burst, Overton hammered four consecutive boundaries off Arshad Khan to inject momentum as the hosts collected 107 runs in the final eight overs.However, the early damage proved too great to overcome, as the recovery ultimately fell short against GT’s well-settled top order.
Article Summary: Gujarat Titans beat Chennai Super Kings by eight wickets. South African paceman Kagiso Rabada (3-25) and in-form ‘home boy’ B Sai Sudharsan (87,46 balls; 4x4, 7x6) starred. CSK now have six from eight and their hopes of a last-four berth are fast diminishing.

Article Title: Ipl 2026: IPL 2026: Yuzvendra Chahal urges bowlers to adapt even as IPL sees new batting peaks
Article Text:
Yuzvendra Chahal (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

NEW DELHI: Yuzvendra Chahal brimmed with confidence after Punjab Kings scripted T20 history at the Arun Jaitley Stadium, pulling off the highest successful chase in men’s T20 cricket by hunting down a daunting 265 against Delhi Capitals.“It will be hard for any team to face our batting line-up,” the veteran leg-spinner said.Chahal stressed that belief coursed through the camp even before a ball was bowled. “We accepted the total and were feeling very positive before the innings. We know the quality we possess with the bat. It was a batting-friendly wicket, and we needed an excellent start if we were to chase such a big score. We got exactly that.”Openers Prabhsimran Singh and Priyansh Arya provided the perfect launchpad, smashing 116 runs in the Powerplay alone. “Prabhsimran and Priyansh gave us the momentum we needed in the first six overs. That helped our batters through the middle overs and put the bowlers under pressure,” Chahal said.While Punjab’s batting unit continues to shine, Chahal acknowledged the mounting challenge bowlers face in high-scoring encounters, especially with modern regulations such as the Impact Sub rule. “I can understand why dealing with the Impact Sub rule can be challenging, but you have to adapt; that’s what the tournament demands. This is the direction the format is heading in, and we have to find solutions because we can’t change it. It becomes even more difficult on smaller grounds.”With 13 points from seven matches, Punjab Kings sit firmly atop the IPL 2026 standings.
Article Summary: Yuzvendra Chahal says Punjab Kings' batting line-up will be hard to face. Punjab Kings record highest chase in men's T20 cricket with 265 against Delhi Capitals. Prabhsimran Singh and Priyansh Arya hit 116 runs in the Powerplay alone.

Article Title: IPL 2026: KL Rahul ditches the anchor tag, scripts masterclass in modern T20 batting
Article Text:
KL Rahul (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

Watch Venugopal Rao explains where Delhi Capitals lost the game while chasing a big total Venugopal Rao explains where Delhi Capitals lost the game while chasing a big total

Poll Should KL Rahul continue to evolve his batting style in T20s? Yes, it's essential for success No, his current style is sufficient

NEW DELHI: For the longest time, KL Rahul ’s T20 career has resembled a man dragging a heavy suitcase. Stuffed inside were the perennial anxieties of strike-rates, the exhausting demands of batting deep, and the archaic, heavy tag of being the “anchor”. On Saturday, at the Arun Jaitley Stadium, Rahul simply set the suitcase aside.What followed was a masterclass in calculated authority. Opening the innings for Delhi Capitals against Punjab Kings, Rahul batted through all 20 overs, remaining unbeaten on a staggering 152 off 67 balls. It was Rahul completely unburdened, accessing all parts of the ground not with reckless abandon but with aggressive clarity.In surging past 150, he etched his name into the annals of the format, registering the highest individual T20 score by an Indian and the third highest in IPL history, trailing only Chris Gayle (175*) and Brendon McCullum (158*).This wasn’t an overnight metamorphosis; it was a conscious, arduous shedding of old habits.“I was very pleased at the end of the first 20 overs,” Rahul said. “It’s something that I’ve been working on for a very long time behind the scenes.”The realization that the T20 format had mutated while he was playing classical notes hit him hard. The game had moved on and Rahul knew he had to catch the train. “For now, it is about doing well in the IPL.Just to step back a little and see where the T20 game has gone. There was a time when the T20 game was slightly different, and I, as an opener, could take my time. But today’s demand is that the first six overs are the most important. The Powerplay is to get as many runs as you can and put the bowling team under pressure,” Rahul said.The transformation required rewiring his muscle memory and his mind, entirely shunning the “we’ll make it up later” philosophy.“I had to sit back and see where I was at,” Rahul said. “In T20 cricket, the mindset that I am in right now is that there is no time to say later. There’s time in ODI cricket, but in T20s there’s no time to think you can go next over. I’ve had to work really hard on my mental set-up.”Crucially, Rahul didn’t abandon his “aesthetic” vibe. He didn’t resort to ugly hoicks to bump his strike-rate. “I stuck to being true to my game, which is to play cricketing shots but also find a way to be aggressive and play proper shots,” he said.The focus, therefore, was firmly on early aggression. “I’ve spoken about six hitting and that’s something that I had to really work on and give myself that sort of freedom to go out there and take on the bowling from maybe ball one, ball two,” he said.Even in a bittersweet context, the magnitude of Rahul’s evolution wasn’t lost in the dressing room. Delhi Capitals’ director of cricket, Venugopal Rao, summed up the monumental effort and said, “In this format, you cannot relax. He took responsibility as a senior player, and you are seeing the difference this year in what he can bring. I am happy for him. We could not get the win, but the way he played till the last over, hats off. ”
Article Summary: KL Rahul scored the highest individual T20 score by an Indian and the third highest in IPL history. Rahul batted through all 20 overs, remaining unbeaten on a staggering 152 off 67 balls. The transformation required rewiring his muscle memory and his mind, entirely shunning the “we’ll make it up later” philosophy.

Article Title: Ipl 2026: Yesterday IPL match result: Rinku Singh, Sunil Narine shine as KKR beat LSG in Super Over
Article Text:
Image credit: BCCI/IPL

Rinku’s knock lifts KKR, Mohsin stars with ball

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

LSG lose way after good start

Strange dismissal adds drama

Standings

Kolkata Knight Riders held their nerve to beat Lucknow Super Giants in a tense Super Over at Ekana Stadium in Lucknow on Sunday. Both teams finished on 155 in their 20 overs, but Sunil Narine made the difference in the decider.Bowling the Super Over, Narine dismissed Nicholas Pooran and Aiden Markram to restrict LSG to just one run - the lowest Super Over total.KKR chased the target of two runs on the first ball, with Rinku Singh hitting a boundary. He also had a great day in the field, taking four catches.Earlier, Rinku’s unbeaten 83 off 51 balls helped KKR post 155 for seven.For LSG, Mohsin Khan was outstanding with the ball, picking up 5 for 23. He removed key players like Ajinkya Rahane, Tim Seifert, Cameron Green, Rovman Powell and Anukul Roy.Despite losing wickets regularly, KKR finished strong as Rinku attacked in the final overs to take the total past 150.Chasing 156, LSG were in a good position at one stage. Rishabh Pant (42) and Markram (31) added 57 runs after the early wicket of Mitchell Marsh.But things changed quickly. Markram got out to a slower ball from Green, while Pant was caught trying an attacking shot against Narine.Young players like Ayush Badoni and Himmat Singh tried to keep the chase alive. In the last over, LSG needed 17 runs. Two no-balls from Kartik Tyagi helped their cause, but he also picked up a wicket.In the end, Mohammed Shami hit a six off the final ball to take the match into a Super Over.There was also an unusual moment during KKR’s innings. Angkrish Raghuvanshi was given out for obstructing the field.He got in the way of a throw from Shami while trying to complete a run. After a review, the third umpire ruled him out, making it a rare dismissal.With this win, KKR moved to eighth place with five points.LSG slipped to the bottom of the table with four points.
Article Summary: Kolkata Knight Riders held their nerve to beat Lucknow Super Giants in a tense Super Over at Ekana Stadium in Lucknow on Sunday. Both teams finished on 155 in their 20 overs, but Sunil Narine made the difference in the decider. Narine dismissed Nicholas Pooran and Aiden Markram to restrict LSG to just one run - the lowest Super Over total. Mohsin Khan was outstanding with the ball, picking up 5 for 23.

Article Title: Why Nicholas Pooran? Rishabh Pant reveals 'group discussion' behind Super Over move
Article Text:
Nicholas Pooran (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

Watch Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling. Bombay Sport Exchange: Munaf Patel on Gautam Gambhir, Virat vs Bumrah & Indian bowling.

Poll Should LSG consider changing their batting order after their recent loss? Yes, they need a new strategy No, stick to the current lineup

NEW DELHI: Mohammed Shami’s late six, which helped take the IPL 2026 clash between Lucknow Super Giants and Kolkata Knight Riders into a Super Over at the Ekana Cricket Stadium in Lucknow on Sunday, ultimately went in vain. KKR sealed the win comfortably in the Super Over. Batting first, LSG lost two wickets for just one run as Sunil Narine dismissed Nicholas Pooran and then Aiden Markram. Chasing a target of two, KKR wrapped up the contest on the very first ball, with Rinku Singh smashing a boundary.The dejection and frustration of letting a win slip away were clearly visible on LSG captain Rishabh Pant ’s face during the post-match presentation.Pant emphasised the need for collective accountability after the defeat against KKR.Chasing 156, LSG finished on 155, with Mohammed Shami smashing a six off the final ball of the innings, but his effort proved insufficient.Speaking after the loss, Pant said the team needs a short break to reset and manage pressure better."I think we definitely need a break. I think we're gonna refresh. There is always pressure, and it's gonna be a pressure game always, but at the same time, we have to look for answers inside, not outside.And just keep it simple. We need to take accountability - every guy. Like, it can't be about one or two guys. It has to be about the whole unit, and a lot of people will take accountability for that for sure," Pant said.Lucknow Super Giants opted to send Nicholas Pooran out to bat in the Super Over despite his poor run of form.The left-hander has struggled in IPL 2026 so far, with his highest score in seven matches being just 22 off 25 balls."We discussed as a group, we had a discussion, and the name that came up was Nicholas Pooran. He might not go through the best form of his life, but at the same time, you will trust your player in a hard situation like this," Pant said.Pant admitted there were no excuses for the result."No excuses there. Just looking at the positive, there might not be enough positive right now, but I think after the break, there will be a lot of enough positive for sure," he said.With this thrilling win, KKR moved to eighth place with five points from eight matches, while LSG slipped to the bottom of the IPL 2026 standings.
Article Summary: Lucknow Super Giants lose to Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2026. LSG lost two wickets for just one run in the Super Over. Captain Rishabh Pant said the team needs a short break to reset.

Article Title: 0, 0, 0, 1, 0: Nicholas Pooran’s Super Over record raises questions over LSG call
Article Text:
Lucknow Super Giants' Nicholas Pooran (PTI Photo/Kamal Kishore)

Watch Ashok Sharma story: Inside the village and house which shaped India's new pace sensation Ashok Sharma story: Inside the village and house which shaped India's new pace sensation

NEW DELHI: Batter Nicholas Pooran ’s Super Over struggles have come under sharp focus after Lucknow Super Giants’ defeat to Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2026.Pooran’s record in tie-breakers makes for grim reading: 0 (5), 0 (1), 0 (1), 1 (2), 0 (1), just one run in 10 balls with three dismissals, raising questions over LSG’s decision to send him in during a high-pressure moment.In Lucknow, where LSG, chasing 156, were pushed into a Super Over after a last-ball six from Mohammed Shami.Opting to bat first, LSG sent Pooran alongside Aiden Markram, but the move backfired instantly.Sunil Narine dismissed Pooran on the first ball and removed Markram soon after, restricting LSG to just one run. KKR chased down the target of two in a single delivery, with Rinku Singh sealing victory.Despite Pooran’s numbers, captain Rishabh Pant defended the call, stressing team backing over individual form."We discussed as a group, we had a discussion, and the name that came up was Nicholas Pooran. He might not go through the best form of his life, but at the same time, you will trust your player in a hard situation like this," Pant said.Pant also called for collective responsibility after the loss."I think we definitely need a break. I think we're gonna refresh. There is always pressure, and it's gonna be a pressure game always, but at the same time, we have to look for answers inside, not outside. And just keep it simple. We need to take accountability - every guy. Like, it can't be about one or two guys. It has to be about the whole unit, and a lot of people will take accountability for that for sure," he said.With LSG now at the bottom of the table, the decision to back Pooran, despite his record, is likely to remain a talking point.
Article Summary: Lucknow Super Giants lose to Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2026. Batter Nicholas Pooran's record in tie-breakers makes for grim reading. Pooran has just one run in 10 balls with three dismissals. LSG captain Rishabh Pant defended the call, stressing team backing.

Article Title: ISL: Mumbai City hold Bengaluru FC to goalless draw
Article Text:
Mumbai City vs Bengaluru FC

Watch ISL Full Highlights: Punjab FC vs Inter Kashi on April 20 ISL Full Highlights: Punjab FC vs Inter Kashi on April 20

BENGALURU: In their final home game of the ISL season, Bengaluru FC dominated possession against Mumbai City FC at the Sree Kanteerava Stadium here on Saturday, but a goalless draw left the Blues frustrated after they failed to convert their chances.It was a similar story for the visitors, who also created openings but lacked the finishing touch in front of goal.The stalemate notwithstanding, the home side acknowledged the 14,723 Blues faithful in attendance. The expressions on several players’ faces reflected the disappointment of dropping points in a crunch fixture.With both teams sharing a point, BFC moved up to fourth place with 16 points, while Mumbai (19) remained third behind leaders Mohun Bagan Super Giant (20) and FC Goa (19). Both teams have three matches left before the season concludes next month.Mumbai threatened midway through the first half, and one early chance fell to Akash Mishra, whose side-footed shot was palmed away to safety.
Article Summary: Bengaluru FC held to a goalless draw by Mumbai City FC at the Sree Kanteerava Stadium. BFC moved up to fourth place with 16 points, while Mumbai (19) remained third behind leaders Mohun Bagan Super Giant and FC Goa.

Article Title: ‘Playing for Barcelona makes you hungry for more’: Dutchwoman Brugts set for Bayern test
Article Text:
Barcelona's Esmee Brugts (AP Photo/Manu Fernandez)

Watch EXCLUSIVE: Where did things go wrong for India in Women's Asia Cup? Aditi Chauhan decodes EXCLUSIVE: Where did things go wrong for India in Women's Asia Cup? Aditi Chauhan decodes

Poll Which team will pose a bigger challenge in the semifinal, Bayern Munich or Real Madrid? Bayern Munich Real Madrid Both have equal challenge

(Fans can watch UEFA Women’s Champions League exclusively on FanCode)

Six months back they clashed in the group stage of the new 18-team single-group format of UEFA Women’s Champions League. Then Barcelona had made a dream start to their European campaign with a 7-1 rout of Bayern Munich.The shapeshifting star of that Oct fixture, Esmee Brugts, who scored a hat-trick while starting the game as a striker and finishing it as a full-back, however believes it might not be as comfortable for the Catalans when the teams face off in the first leg of the semifinal in Munich.“The first game against them was a while ago, and with a different context. We know it will never be the same again. And that’s football. Every game is different and we have to be as prepared as we can, that’s what we train for,” Brugts said during a media interaction as her team stands on the verge of a sixth consecutive UWCL final.The 22-year-old Dutchwoman, who joined Barcelona from PSV Eindhoven in 2023, has been an integral part of Spanish coach Pere Romeu’s scheme this season, where they lost just one match to clinch their seventh Liga F title on the trot on Wednesday.The Catalans have also not lost any UWCL game since their nightmarish summit clash last season against Arsenal, where they had 20 shots on target against the English club’s eight, yet lost 1-0 to a decisive goal from Stina Blackstenius.This time they reached the semifinal by topping the group stage and beating rivals Real Madrid twice in the quarterfinal, with the second leg a return for the team to Camp Nou and Brugts having scored in both legs.Having all her role models — Dutch striker Lieke Martens, Ronaldinho and Neymar — with a Catalan link, and as a child, having famously asked for a Camp Nou tour on her birthday, Brugts acknowledged “her privilege” in striving for the Spanish club and her focus towards winning her second UWCL title with them, which will be the fourth for the Spanish champions.“Playing in Barcelona makes you hungry. As a top athlete, you are hungry to go for more. I think I’m surrounded by such players that are also never satisfied with what we have. We always want more and I learn so much by being surrounded by these teammates,” said the youngster, who has been nominated by UEFA as one of the Breakthrough Players of UWCL 2025-26.With the likes of the recovering Aitana Bonmati and Alexia Putellas up front, Brugts has made a place for herself as a versatile “playmaking defender” — having already played as winger, wing-back, right-back and full-back in Romeu’s setup.“I have several roles, it depends really on the plan of the game,” she explained.“I remember my first goal against Bayern Munich. I had the freedom to play inside more like a midfielder, you could say. This is Barca, though, and we are all very free to play where we can. It also depends on Pere (Romeu), where he wants to utilise me in each game. Yeah, I can play on the outside, inside, further up the field, it all depends,” she added.Having scored 10 goals so far in all competitions — with four of them in UWCL — and provided six assists, the Dutch international has already proved her value to her team.And with Brugts confirming the return of three-time Ballon D’Or winner Bonmati to Barcelona’s training sessions after a five-month injury hiatus, the Blaugranes will be eager to make their second sojourn at the Allianz Arena a memorable one.“We are quite happy with how it’s been so far,” she said, “But I’d say something that describes how we are in Barcelona. We are never satisfied with what we have, we always want more.”
Article Summary: Barcelona face Bayern Munich in the semifinal of UEFA Women's Champions League. Esmee Brugts scored a hat-trick in Barcelona's 7-1 win over Bayern in October. The 22-year-old has been an integral part of Spanish coach Pere Romeu’s scheme this season.

Article Title: Big blow for Spain: Injured Lamine Yamal ruled out for season ahead of FIFA World Cup
Article Text:
Lamine Yamal

Lamine Yamal has suffered a hamstring injury and will miss the rest of the season, but his club FC Barcelona said on Thursday that he is expected to be fit in time for the upcoming FIFA World Cup."Tests have confirmed that first-team player Lamine Yamal has a hamstring injury in his left leg," Barcelona said.Spain begin their Group H campaign against Cape Verde on June 15, followed by matches against Saudi Arabia on June 21 and Uruguay six days later.Yamal picked up the injury during Barcelona’s 1-0 win over Celta Vigo in La Liga on Wednesday."This injury is going to keep me off the pitch just when I wanted to be there the most, and that hurts even more than I can put into words," Yamal wrote on social media."This isn't the end, it's just a break. I'll come back stronger, with even more determination than ever, and next season will be better."The 18-year-old, who has scored six goals in 25 matches for Spain since his debut in 2023, won and scored a penalty in that game to seal the result.After converting the spot-kick past Ionut Radu, Yamal went down and asked for treatment, staying on the ground as teammates came to celebrate.He was later substituted, with Roony Bardghji coming on in his place."He wouldn't leave the pitch without reason," said Barcelona coach Hansi Flick. "As a coach you have to manage it, you have to live with it. It's a pity that Lamine is injured now."Barcelona’s win took them nine points ahead of Real Madrid at the top of the table with six matches left, including a Clasico on May 10.Yamal is the club’s top scorer this season with 24 goals, despite missing games in September and October due to a groin issue.He was also part of Spain’s Euro 2024-winning squad and had recently spoken about his aim to play in the World Cup.
Article Summary: Lamine Yamal has been ruled out for the rest of the season with a hamstring injury. The 18-year-old picked up the injury during Barcelona's 1-0 win over Celta Vigo on Wednesday. He has scored six goals in 25 matches for Spain since his debut in 2023.

Article Title: 'Moral bankruptcy of US': Tehran hits back after Donald Trump envoy suggests replacing Iran with Italy at FIFA World Cup
Article Text:
US President Donald Trump answers questions from reporters on the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the Oval Office of the White House, Monday, Nov. 17, 2025, in Washington, as FIFA president Gianni Infantino listens. (AP)

An envoy to US President Donald Trump has asked world football body FIFA to consider replacing Iran with Italy at the upcoming World Cup, according to the Financial Times.US special envoy Paolo Zampolli told the FT, as quoted by news agency AFP, that it would be a "dream" to see Italy play at the World Cup in the United States, Mexico and Canada, even though they lost in a qualification playoff last month.The Iranian embassy in Rome responded by saying the idea showed America's "moral bankruptcy" and added that Italy did not need "political privileges" to prove its football record.The FT report said the suggestion was also linked to efforts to ease tensions between Trump and Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni after differences between them over comments related to Pope Leo XIV and the Iran war."I confirm I have suggested to Trump and (FIFA President Gianni) Infantino that Italy replace Iran at the World Cup. I'm an Italian native and it would be a dream to see the Azzurri at a US-hosted tournament. With four titles, they have the pedigree to justify inclusion," Zampolli said.Italy have won the World Cup four times, but failed to qualify for a third straight edition after losing a penalty shootout to Bosnia and Herzegovina in their playoff final."Italy has earned its greatness in football on the pitch, not thanks to political privileges," the Iranian embassy said on X."The attempt to exclude Iran from the World Cup only reveals the 'moral bankruptcy' of the United States, which is afraid even of the presence of eleven young Iranians on the field of play."Iran's participation in the World Cup has been thrown into doubt by the war with the US and Israel that broke out on February 28.The Iranian football federation (FFIRI) had said in April that it was in talks with FIFA to shift its World Cup matches from the United States to Mexico.FIFA president Gianni Infantino had said last month, during Iran’s friendly match against Costa Rica in Turkey, that Iran would take part in the World Cup and would play "where they are supposed to be, according to the draw".
Article Summary: US special envoy Paolo Zampolli said it would be a "dream" to see Italy play at the World Cup in the United States, Mexico and Canada. The Iranian embassy in Rome responded by saying the idea showed America's "moral bankruptcy"

Article Title: SAFF Women's Championship: Pakistan pull out of India-hosted football tournament
Article Text:
Representative image

Watch ISL Full Highlights: Punjab FC vs Inter Kashi on April 20 ISL Full Highlights: Punjab FC vs Inter Kashi on April 20

The draw:

NEW DELHI: Pakistan’s women’s football team has pulled out of the 2026 SAFF Women’s Championship, hosted by India, the Pakistan Football Federation (PFF) said on Thursday, as reported by news agency IANS.The South Asian Football Federation (SAFF) had earlier said that all seven member teams would take part in the tournament, "making it one of the premier women’s football events in the region."With Pakistan’s withdrawal, the tournament will now be played with six teams. The event is scheduled at the Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium in Margao, Goa, from May 25 to June 6.This will be the second time India hosts the SAFF Women’s Championship after the 2016 edition in Siliguri, where India won their fourth title. It will also be the second SAFF tournament in Goa. The men’s SAFF Championship in 1999 was also held at the same venue, where India beat Bangladesh in the final.After Pakistan’s exit, the remaining six teams — Nepal, India (hosts), Sri Lanka, Bangladesh (defending champions), Bhutan and Maldives — have been placed into two groups following the draw held at the SAFF Secretariat in Dhaka.The withdrawal affects Pakistan women’s football, which had been looking to gain more match experience after facing administrative issues and limited international games in recent years.Pakistan had also pulled out of the men’s junior hockey World Cup held in Tamil Nadu in November last year. They did not send their senior men’s team to the Asia Cup in Rajgir as well.India are the most successful team in the SAFF Women’s Championship, winning five titles in 2010, 2012, 2014, 2016 and 2019. Bangladesh have won the last two editions in 2022 and 2024.Group A: Nepal, Sri Lanka, BhutanGroup B: India, Bangladesh, Maldives.
Article Summary: Pakistan's women's football team pulls out of 2026 SAFF Women's Championship. The tournament will now be played with six teams. The event is scheduled at the Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium in Margao, Goa, from May 25 to June 6.

Article Title: Fact check: Inter Kashi not paying players and staff?
Article Text:
Inter Kashi are eighth in the ISL points table. (Image: AIFF)

New Delhi:

Watch ISL Full Highlights: Punjab FC vs Inter Kashi on April 20 ISL Full Highlights: Punjab FC vs Inter Kashi on April 20

Antonio Habas. (ISL Photo)

Antonio Habas used strong words after Inter Kashi went down 0-3 to Punjab FC in the Indian Super League (ISL) on April 20. The Inter Kashi manager congratulated Punjab FC after a strong show with goals from Samir Zeljkovic, Effiong Nsungusi Jr., and Bede Osuji. But his comments toward his own team raised eyebrows."I want to say congratulations to Punjab FC, but I don’t want to talk about the match because Inter Kashi is in a very, very complicated situation with a lot of problems in the club. I don’t want to talk about the match," said Habas."We need to talk about the situation of the players and staff with the owner of the club, because it’s impossible to compete in the ISL. This is my answer today.""I am very, very sad about this situation. We have been fighting since last season 100%, and now it’s possible that the players and staff will not receive their salaries. It’s impossible," he continued.An Inter Kashi insider clarified to TimesofIndia.com that there are no financial issues within the club. Another agent informed us that they have no information on the matter.The club source suggested that the issue Habas is pointing to could well be their ongoing legal tussle with Churchill Brothers.That process has seen Inter Kashi and Churchill Brothers approach the All India Football Federation (AIFF), the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) and now the Delhi High Court.The two clubs have been locked in a legal battle over last year's I-League title. After Churchill were crowned champions, and literally handed over the trophy, the title was awarded to Inter Kashi following a disciplinary hearing. CAS had ruled in favour of Inter Kashi, and the club were promoted to the ISL.Churchill, however, attempted to gain entry as late as after the start of the ISL season, but their efforts were unsuccessful. The club also opted against playing in the second division (now called the Indian Football League).On March 31, the AIFF had accused its Executive Committee member and Churchill Brothers CEO Valanka Alemao of exerting “sustained pressure” to facilitate their inclusion in the ISL.The statement came on the day Valanka accused AIFF president Kalyan Chaubey, vice president NA Haris and deputy secretary general M Satyanarayan of intimidation and harassment during the Executive Committee meeting.Meanwhile, Inter Kashi's first season in the ISL has, expectedly, been a challenge. It took them until the third game for their first win of the season, and the loss to Punjab halted their streak of two victories.They're eighth in the ISL points table with 11 points after nine matches played, clinching three wins along the way. Their next game is against NorthEast United FC on May 2.
Article Summary: Inter Kashi lost 0-3 to Punjab FC in the Indian Super League (ISL) on April 20. Inter Kashi are eighth in the ISL points table with 11 points after nine matches played. Their next game is against NorthEast United FC on May 2. The two clubs are in a legal battle over last year's I-League title.

Article Title: Manchester City: Manchester City leapfrog Arsenal in Premier League title race; Burnley relegated
Article Text:
Manchester City's Erling Haaland, second right, scores his side's goal against Burnley. (AP Photo)

Haaland delivers, City seize control

Burnley relegated after dismal run

Race tightens, drama builds

Manchester City edged ahead in the Premier League title race after a hard-fought 1-0 win over Burnley FC at Turf Moor, a result that not only sent Pep Guardiola’s side top but also confirmed Burnley’s relegation.A first-half strike from Erling Haaland proved decisive as City moved above Arsenal FC on goals scored, with both teams locked on points and five matches remaining in a gripping title battle.City, fresh from a crucial win over Arsenal days earlier, started with intent. Within five minutes, Haaland capitalised on a precise pass from Jérémy Doku, calmly chipping the ball over goalkeeper Martin Dúbravka to hand the visitors an early lead.The defending champions dominated proceedings, repeatedly testing Dubravka, while Rayan Cherki came close with a long-range effort that rattled the woodwork. Despite controlling the game, City failed to extend their advantage, much to the frustration of manager Pep Guardiola.“The chances were there. We created a lot. We made a fantastic game,” Guardiola said, while also expressing disappointment at his side’s inability to convert dominance into a bigger margin.Burnley nearly punished that profligacy before half-time when Zian Flemming dragged a golden opportunity wide. Haaland later struck the post after the break, but City had to settle for a narrow win.The defeat confirmed Burnley’s relegation to the Championship for the third time in five seasons. Managed by Scott Parker, the Clarets have endured a torrid campaign, collecting just 20 points from 34 matches.“We’ve fallen a bit short… in certain moments we’ve lacked quality,” Parker admitted, as his side slipped 13 points from safety with only four games left.Burnley’s drop continues a pattern of promotion and relegation struggles, highlighting the gap between the Premier League and the second tier.At the top, City’s victory sets up a tense finale. Although they now lead the standings, Guardiola’s men face a tougher run-in compared to Arsenal, keeping the title race finely balanced.Elsewhere, Leeds United boosted their survival hopes with a late 2-2 draw against AFC Bournemouth, while Bournemouth’s push for European qualification took a hit.
Article Summary: Erling Haaland scores only goal of the game as Manchester City beat Burnley FC 1-0 at Turf Moor. Defeat confirms Burnley's relegation to the Championship for the third time in five seasons. Leeds United and AFC Bournemouth play out 2-2 draw at Elland Road.

Article Title: Chelsea sack coach Liam Rosenior as Blues hit historic low with five straight EPL defeats
Article Text:
Chelsea's head coach Liam Rosenior (AP Photo)

Historic slump triggers ruthless call

Financial strain, dressing room cracks

Chelsea FC have sacked head coach Liam Rosenior less than four months into his tenure, bringing a swift and dramatic end to a spell marred by historic underachievement.The decision came in the wake of a 3-0 defeat to Brighton & Hove Albion — a result Rosenior himself branded “unacceptable” — and a dire run that has seen Chelsea lose five consecutive Premier League matches without scoring. It marks the club’s worst such streak since 1912, underlining the scale of the collapse.“Chelsea Football Club has today parted company with Head Coach Liam Rosenior,” the club said in a statement, admitting performances had “fallen below the necessary standards” despite the season still being alive.Rosenior, 41, had arrived in January from RC Strasbourg Alsace following the exit of Enzo Maresca. While his appointment raised eyebrows, early promise quickly gave way to a staggering downturn.Chelsea have now lost seven of their last eight matches in all competitions, including a heavy Champions League exit to Paris Saint-Germain and a League Cup elimination at the hands of Arsenal FC.The Blues currently sit seventh in the table, seven points off the Champions League places with just four games remaining — far from expectations for a squad assembled at enormous cost.Assistant coach Calum McFarlane has been placed in interim charge, beginning with a crucial FA Cup semi-final clash against Leeds United. His immediate task will be to salvage European qualification and ease the financial burden after Chelsea reported a staggering £262.4 million pre-tax loss last season.Behind the scenes, tensions had been brewing. Questions over the earlier sacking of Maresca were publicly raised by senior players including Enzo Fernández and Marc Cucurella, while inconsistent performances further strained relations between the squad and Rosenior.The club’s ownership group, led by Todd Boehly, has now dismissed five permanent managers in under five seasons, raising serious concerns about long-term stability despite over £1 billion spent on players.Chelsea confirmed they will begin a “process of self-reflection” to appoint a long-term successor, with names like Andoni Iraola, Marco Silva and Edin Terzic already linked.
Article Summary: Chelsea have sacked head coach Liam Rosenior less than four months into his tenure. The decision came in the wake of a 3-0 defeat to Brighton & Hove Albion. Chelsea have now lost seven of their last eight matches in all competitions.

Article Title: Mohammedan Sporting: ISL: A well-earned point for Mohammedan Sporting against Chennaiyin FC
Article Text:
Mohammedan Sporting vs Chennaiyin FC match. (Pic credit: ISL)

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

Watch Football returns to Delhi! SC Delhi gear up for first ISL home game Football returns to Delhi! SC Delhi gear up for first ISL home game

CHENNAI: After all they have been through this season, Mohammedan Sporting will head home satisfied with a 0-0 draw against Chennaiyin FC in the Indian Super League at the Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium on Tuesday. A single point did not do much to their position as relegation-threatened Md Sporting remained last in the table with two points from nine matches, while CFC are ninth with nine points.Chennaiyin started strongly but Md Sporting grew into the game with a sustained spell of attack after the 20-minute mark. It began with a series of three corners, the last of which saw Makan Chothe whip in a curling cross from the left towards an unmarked Hira Mondal. At the far post, the defender stretched to guide the ball goalward but goalkeeper Mohammed Nawaz produced a fingertip save to deny the effort.Nawaz came to Chennaiyin’s rescue on a couple of occasions and kept the score board in check. Meanwhile, despite creating chances, CFC lacked the finishing touch in the final third.In the second half, Chennaiyin attempted to build from the back but Md Sporting stood firm with a well-organised back four. Midfielder Alberto Noguera dropped deep, which limited his creative influence. CFC coach Clifford Miranda turned to his bench, bringing on fresh legs Inigo Martin and ‘local lad’ S. Prakadeswaran, yet the home side struggled to break through.
Article Summary: Mohammedan Sporting draw 0-0 with Chennaiyin FC in Indian Super League. Md Sporting remain last in the table with two points from nine matches, while CFC are ninth with nine points. CFC coach Clifford Miranda brought on fresh legs Inigo Martin and ‘local lad’ S. Prakadeswaran.

Article Title: Lamine Yamal: ‘Hope to achieve half of what Messi did’: Lamine Yamal aims to follow legend’s footsteps
Article Text:
Lamine Yamal at 2026 Laureus World Sports Awards ceremony in Madrid. (AP Photo)

Go Beyond The Boundary with our YouTube channel.

MADRID: At just 18 years of age, Lamine Yamal is already being spoken of as football’s next global superstar, though his feet remain firmly on the ground. The Rocafonda-born winger, raised just outside Barcelona, has enjoyed a meteoric rise with FC Barcelona, playing a pivotal role in a remarkable 2025 season that saw the club secure La Liga, the Spanish Super Cup, and the Copa del Rey, alongside a deep run in the UEFA Champions League Such success has inevitably drawn comparisons to Lionel Messi , the iconic figure who defined an era at Barcelona across 21 years. Yet Yamal has been quick in rejecting those parallels. For him, Messi is untouchable.Speaking after receiving his second successive honour at the Laureus World Sports Awards, this time as ‘World Young Sportsperson of the Year’, Yamal expressed deep humility.“For me, Messi is the greatest football player in history. I hope I can follow in his footsteps but I’d already be grateful to achieve even half of what he has.“He is a legend and I don’t find myself worthy compared with him. I only hope to achieve half of what he has done, he is without a doubt the best in the history of football,” he said.“When you realise an athlete isn’t just a legend of their sport but of all sports... Messi, who for me is the best player in history, and if he isn’t the greatest athlete ever, he’s definitely in that conversation with all of them. He’s more than an idol.I think everyone respects him for everything he’s done. He’s been part of the childhood of every kid when we played in the park or at school and I hope I’ll be able to follow in his footsteps,” added Yamal.Despite the accolades, Yamal is acutely aware of the responsibility that comes with fame at such a young age. “It’s hard to realise you are a role model and that you can’t make mistakes,” he admitted,“But I see it as something positive. Whenever I send a message, I try to make it meaningful and helpful.”Yamal embraced the evolving relationship between players and fans on social media. “People are entertained by what you do off the pitch too and I like this change. This has strengthened my bond with fans.”Yamal’s ambitions remain clear to fulfill his childhood dream of playing in the World Cup this summer. “I’ve always imagined playing in a World Cup, seeing my mother in the stands, representing my country. I hope we can win it,” he said.
Article Summary: Lamine Yamal was named World Young Sportsperson of the Year at the Laureus World Sports Awards in Madrid. The 18-year-old winger has enjoyed a meteoric rise with FC Barcelona. Yamal has been compared to Barcelona legend Lionel Messi. The winger is aware of the responsibility that comes with fame.

Article Title: Diego Pavia: Diego Pavia’s alleged ‘vulgar’ post fuels backlash as he ends up doing something unseen in 12 years
Article Text:
Diego Pavia’s alleged ‘vulgar’ post fuels backlash as he ends up doing something unseen in 12 years (Getty)

Viral post storm and rare draft outcome put Diego Pavia in the spotlight
